about.title: We build tools people love to use.
about.subtitle: A small team with a big goal: ship software that makes a difference.
about.missionText: Create digital tools that solve real problems in a simple, elegant way.
about.visionText: Become the go-to platform for teams that want to ship fast and well.
about.valuesText: Transparency, speed, quality and obsession with user experience.
about.teamSubtitle: A distributed team working from anywhere in the world.

pricing.title: Simple, clear plans
pricing.subtitle: Pick the plan that fits you. No surprises.

faq.subtitle: Quick answers about the template, deploy, languages and customisation.

plantilla.a: A ready-to-go base for modern sites with SvelteKit 2 and Svelte 5: UI components (shadcn-svelte), SEO, i18n, dark mode and deployment tuned for Vercel or Netlify.

principiante.q: Do I need a lot of front-end experience?
principiante.a: You don’t have to be an expert: tweak JSON copy, CSS colours and copy blocks from the component gallery. When you want to go further, the code is organised to scale without mess.

comercial.q: Can I use it for commercial projects?
comercial.a: Yes. The template is MIT: use it for clients, products or landings with no paid licence restrictions.

deploy.q: How do I deploy to production?
deploy.a: Push the repo to GitHub and connect the project to Vercel or Netlify. Build is npm run build; set PUBLIC_SITE_URL for SEO (sitemap and Open Graph).

i18n.a: Spanish and English with keys under src/lib/i18n. To add another language, add a JSON file, register translations and extend the locale type in the i18n module.

cms.a: Yes. The repo includes optional Sanity pieces; you can also consume any API (Strapi, Contentful, local Markdown) from load or endpoints.

seo.a: Centralised metadata (setSeo), JSON-LD, sitemap, robots and security headers in hooks.server.ts. Set titles and descriptions per route.

rendimiento.a: Svelte compiles to small JS; use optimised images, lazy loading where it matters and Vite’s bundle analyser as the app grows.

ssr.title: Data loading demo
ssr.intro: Data comes from src/routes/components/+page.ts with load(). Same route as the demo form (+page.server.ts).
ssr.howBody:
1. +page.ts exports load()
2. It runs on the server
3. Data reaches +page.svelte via props
4. The component receives them with $props()

facts.compiler: SvelteKit compiles your code on the server

blogPost.title: My first post with SvelteKit
blogPost.lead: Sample blog with Markdown/mdsvex (previously its own route; now a gallery demo).
blogPost.p1: Example of Markdown and mdsvex on the same page.
blogPost.h2: How to create a new post
blogPost.ol2: Add a +page.svx file inside that folder
blogPost.ol4: Add frontmatter with title, date and description
blogPost.li1: Speed: pages can be generated at build time
blogPost.li2: Markdown: write plain text, it becomes HTML
blogPost.li3: Components: embed Svelte components inside Markdown
blogPost.li4: SEO: each post can have its own title and description

seo.title: My SvelteKit Starter — Svelte 5 + Kit 2 template ready to ship
seo.description: All in one repo: shadcn-style UI you own, Tailwind v4, TypeScript, ES/EN copy, dark mode, technical SEO and demo pages. For vibe coders who skip reinventing the stack.

hero.title: The SvelteKit starter for people who just want to build
hero.subtitle: Runes, routes, forms, toasts, light/dark theme and a component gallery living in your repo—not an opaque package. Fork, tweak, deploy.

performance.title: Lean JS in the browser
performance.desc: Svelte compiles components to small output — great for landings and apps where payload matters.

types.title: TypeScript that survives CI
types.desc: Structured for `npm run check`: typed routes and UI so failures happen in the pipeline, not on users.

dx.title: shadcn-style UI in your repo
dx.desc: Buttons, cards, forms live under `src/lib/components/ui/` — edit the source, no black-box dependency.

deploy.title: SEO & production baseline
deploy.desc: Meta tags, JSON-LD, `/sitemap.xml`, `/robots.txt`, dynamic OG — wire the repo to Vercel or Netlify when your domain is ready.

cms.title: Sanity CMS (optional)
cms.desc: Studio + schema under `sanity/`, server client + GROQ helpers under `src/lib/server/sanity/`. Enable with `SANITY_PROJECT_ID` and `SANITY_DATASET` in `.env`.

product.title: Product-ready UX
product.desc: ES/EN strings via `$t()`, light/dark theme (mode-watcher), cookie banner and JSON-LD — a usable landing, not an isolated mock.

step1.title: Get the code
step1.desc: Clone the repo (`git clone …`) or download the ZIP from GitHub. This template already includes SvelteKit, Tailwind v4, TypeScript and the UI setup.

step2.title: Install dependencies
step2.desc: From the project folder run `npm install`. Requires Node.js 22+ (see `package.json`).

step3.title: Start the dev server
step3.desc: Run `npm run dev` and open the URL printed in the terminal (usually http://localhost:5173).

step4.title: Customize & deploy
step4.desc: Update name and links in project config, adjust copy and theme colors, run `npm run build`, then ship to Vercel, Netlify or your host.

cta.title: What’s next?
cta.subtitle: Explore the gallery, steal UI patterns and swap in your brand—this template is the launchpad, not the ceiling.

hero.subtitle: Design and ship clean, fast, elegant interfaces with a premium visual system and a modern product experience.
hero.footnote: No generic templates. Modern product sections ready to combine, animate, and ship to production.

in hours, not weeks.

usecases.eyebrow: The UI toolkit that changes everything

superpowers.eyebrow: Explore Svelte Starter in a whole new way.
superpowers.title: Everyday superpowers for modern product interfaces.
superpowers.card1Desc: Typography, spacing, and radius scales that stay consistent from day one, without wrestling a massive design system.
superpowers.card2Desc: Scroll reveal, hover states, and polished micro-interactions are already wired and easy to tune.
superpowers.card3Desc: Sections designed like building blocks: hero, pricing, FAQ, and more, all fitting together without breaking layout rhythm.
superpowers.card4Desc: Runes-based state, actions, and TypeScript typing so developer experience feels as modern as the UI.
superpowers.card5Desc: Strong defaults for spacing and visual rhythm, with clean CSS you can adapt to your brand in minutes.
superpowers.card6Desc: Built for real products, not just portfolios: loading states, empty states, and error messaging included.

features.title: Designed for Modern Interfaces
features.subtitle: Production-ready components with polished aesthetics, modern performance, and a consistent visual experience across your product.

cards.cleanDesc: Build clean, modern interfaces with simple, reusable components.
cards.fastDesc: Svelte compiles your code for speed, so your UI stays fast by default.
cards.premiumDesc: Minimal visual style, expressive typography, and refined micro-interactions.
cards.customDesc: Adapt color, typography, and spacing with design tokens in seconds.

pricing.subtitle: Choose the plan that fits your team and growth stage.
pricing.starterDesc: Perfect for side projects and early MVPs.
pricing.proDesc: The best plan for teams shipping production products.
pricing.teamsDesc: For product teams moving fast across multiple products.

testimonials.title: Start bringing balance back to your interface work.

quotes.q1: We moved from static marketing pages to complete SvelteKit flows in days. Svelte Starter gave us a consistent visual language without fighting Figma in every iteration.
quotes.q2: As a freelancer, I finally have a serious starting point. I reuse the same foundation across clients, while each project still feels unique through sections and tokens.
quotes.q3: Our product team can prototype new ideas directly in SvelteKit without waiting for hand-coded HTML every time. The micro-interactions feel right out of the box.
quotes.q4: Since migrating our marketing site to Svelte Starter, the team stopped saying 'we will build it tomorrow' and started saying 'we can ship it today 🚀'.
quotes.q5: We use it as the foundation for three different products and almost nobody notices. We swap tokens, logos, and copy, and it just works. It feels like a living design system ✨.
quotes.q6: Every sprint starts by cloning a Svelte Starter layout instead of reusing an old page. That alone saves hours of design debates 🙌.
quotes.q7: Our remote team shares landing screenshots in Slack like they are new Dribbble shots. The best part is that everything is live in production 😍.
quotes.q8: We built a lightweight design system on top of Svelte Starter and, for the first time, design and code move at the same speed. No more endless pixel-perfect loops 🧩.
quotes.q9: The client thought we hired an expensive agency. In reality, it was Svelte Starter layouts with a few brand customizations 😏.
quotes.q10: Svelte Starter lets us keep a coherent component library across product, marketing, and internal dashboards without duplicating work ⚙️.
quotes.q11: It feels like opening a mature app from day one. We only had to plug in our data and start iterating.
quotes.q12: We used to have four versions of the same button. Now everything comes from one kit, and support sends us hearts in Slack 💜.
quotes.q13: I never thought I would enjoy building tables and settings screens this much. Svelte Starter makes even routine screens feel premium 😅.
quotes.q14: We moved from 'do not touch that fragile view' to 'clone the Svelte Starter pattern and adapt it.' It is a completely different mindset 💡.
quotes.q15: Backend handoff is now just sharing components. Fewer tickets, fewer screenshots, and many more happy pull requests 🤝.
quotes.q16: Some motion details feel straight out of apps like Linear or Superlist. Best of all, the CSS stays clean and readable 👀.
quotes.q17: We turned a Figma prototype into a navigable demo in one afternoon. The sales team sent us a very enthusiastic THANK YOU 🔥.
quotes.q18: This landing let us validate our product messaging in days. We only changed copy and data, the design foundation was already there.
quotes.q19: Navigating Svelte Starter feels like the best modern productivity apps, while remaining pure Svelte code we fully control.
quotes.q20: This landing let us validate our product messaging in days. We only changed copy and data, while the design foundation was already done ✅.
quotes.q21: We handed it to marketing and they now create new pages on their own. I just review the pull request and smile 😎.
quotes.q22: Svelte Starter code is so clean that even our very detail-oriented CTO gave us an immediate green light 🧠.
quotes.q23: In investor demos, we used a landing built with Svelte Starter directly. Nobody asked about design quality, only about the roadmap. That says it all 💬.
quotes.q24: We connected Svelte Starter to our API and had a client mini-portal in one afternoon that looked polished for months ✨.
quotes.q25: The data team asked for 'something quick' to present metrics. We gave them a Svelte Starter dashboard and now they do not want anything else 📊.
quotes.q26: I love being able to open the landing repo, tweak a few props, and see real changes in minutes. Zero fear of breaking the design 💻.

deepDetails.eyebrow: Details that matter for product UI.
deepDetails.title: Move seamlessly from idea to shipped interface.
deepDetails.subtitle: Svelte Starter handles the details that shape great product UI: states, spacing, visual rhythm, and accessibility.

items.item2Text: Repeatable sections for pricing, onboarding, and in-product announcements.

items.item4Text: Widget-style cards ready for metrics, activity feeds, and operational views.
items.item4Tag: Blocks that feel like product UI
items.item5Text: Nested layouts designed for complex views without sacrificing clarity.
items.item5Tag: Complex under the hood, clear on the surface

items.item6Tag: Developer experience and design in sync
items.item7Text: Clean integrations with REST and GraphQL APIs.
